vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1368

plan.title: monitoring: long-running monitors using pr_merge_readiness are unreliable in Grok Build due to harness fragility

narratives.Description: monitoring: long-running monitors using pr_merge_readiness are unreliable in Grok Build due to harness fragility. When the underlying gh capture decodes badly, the readiness script returns no JSON and dependent monitors see head: None forever, even though the actual PR state is determinable via REST or check runs.
narratives.Overview:
## Problem

During the 1166 swarm merge cascade, a background monitor was launched to automatically merge #1363 (once it became mergeable after rebase) and then the Wave 3 PRs.

The monitor kept reporting for ~15+ minutes:

> #1363 mergeable_state = blocked
> (last reviewed: none, head: None)

Even though:
- The rebase and force-push had succeeded.
- A direct manual check confirmed the head was correct and the state was genuinely "blocked" (required checks hadn't run yet on the new commit).

The monitor was effectively blind the entire time.

## Root Cause

The monitor script depended on repeatedly calling:

```bash
python scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py <N> --repo deftai/directive --json
```

This script (and similar tools) internally uses `subprocess.run(..., capture_output=True, text=True)` to call `gh`.

In the Grok Build harness on Windows, this frequently triggers:

```
Exception in thread Thread-3 (_readerthread):
...
UnicodeDecodeError: 'cp1252' codec can't decode byte ...
```

(See also #1353 and #1366 for the broader class of harness output capture problems.)

When this happens:
- The readiness script returns no valid JSON.
- Any dependent monitor sees `head: None` and cannot make progress decisions.

This is not a bug in the monitor logic itself — it is being fed broken data by an unreliable underlying tool in this environment.

## Impact

- Reliable long-running automation (merge cascades, review monitoring, etc.) becomes extremely difficult.
- The hybrid Grok Build swarm path (`spawn_subagent`) already has visibility challenges (#1365). This makes monitoring even worse.
- We end up having to fall back to manual checks and human intervention even when we tried to automate.

## Proposed Solutions

1. **Make monitoring tools resilient (high priority)**
   - `pr_merge_readiness.py` and similar scripts should have multiple fallback paths:
     - Primary: current logic
     - Fallback 1: Direct `gh api` REST calls + manual parsing of the Greptile comment (bypassing the full script when it fails)
     - Fallback 2: Simple `gh pr view` + check-run status as a last resort (with clear warnings)
   - Always return structured output even on partial failure (e.g. `{ "error": "...", "partial_data": {...} }`).

2. **Create a safe monitoring helper**
   - Add something like `scripts/monitor_pr.py` or a small library that long-running monitors can use.
   - It should handle the common failure modes gracefully and provide reliable "current state" information.

3. **Better patterns for the swarm skill**
   - Document recommended ways to build reliable long-running monitors in the Grok Build hybrid path.
   - Consider adding built-in support for "merge when ready" style automation that is resilient to these harness issues.

4. **Platform-side (Grok Build)**
   - Improve subprocess output capture reliability on Windows (UTF-8 + better error handling in reader threads).
   - This is the same underlying class of problem reported in #1353.

## References

- This specific occurrence: 1166 swarm auto-merge monitor for #1363 (after rebase).
- #1353 (original harness Windows capture issues)
- #1365 (sub-agents going dark / visibility problems)
- #1366 (harness UnicodeDecodeError breaking monitoring tools)
- The monitor script and repeated "head: None" failures observed live during this session.

This makes the current hybrid swarm + review-cycle workflow significantly less reliable for anything that requires waiting on external state (Greptile, CI, etc.).
narratives.Cluster: Wave 2 (monitor reliability, depends on #1366 Wave 1) of the Grok Build harness fragility cluster. Sibling Wave 2: #1365 (sub-agent visibility, parallelizable). Wave 3 dependent: #1369 (cascade automation). Cross-ref #1353. Cluster tracked via the `cluster:grok-harness` tag across four vBRIEFs (no GitHub umbrella issue filed).
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm monitor running cascade automation on Grok Build, I want pr_merge_readiness.py to keep returning structured output through transient gh failures, so that long-running monitors never blind on a single decode crash or partial response.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Add a layered fallback chain to scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py: primary path stays as-is (Greptile rolling-summary comment parsing), fallback 1 calls gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<N> (REST) and parses the comment list directly, fallback 2 uses gh pr view <N> --json state + check-run status as a coarse last-resort signal. Each layer emits structured JSON with a `via` discriminator.
2. Ensure every failure path returns structured JSON (never raw exception text); partial failures emit { error, partial_data, via } so a monitor can keep stepping forward instead of going blind.
3. Add scripts/monitor_pr.py as a thin 'wait until ready' helper that loops pr_merge_readiness.py with adaptive cadence (1m -> 3m -> 5m), tolerates fallback responses, and exits when the canonical CLEAN exit fires.
4. Update skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 5/6 to cite scripts/monitor_pr.py as the resilient long-running monitor surface and document what each `via` value implies (fallback2 is a coarse signal, not a CLEAN verdict).
5. Add tests/cli/test_pr_merge_readiness_fallbacks.py covering primary success, primary-failure -> fallback1, primary+fallback1-failure -> fallback2, and total-failure (structured error). Tests route subprocess capture through the #1366 helper.
6. Add CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry referencing #1368 and the #1366 dependency.
narratives.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1368 and the #1166 swarm cascade monitor failure on PR #1363. Depends on #1366 safe-subprocess helper.

narrative.Acceptance: When the primary parse path raises or returns empty, the script falls back to REST + manual parsing (fallback1), then to a coarse PR-view + check-run signal (fallback2), and every response emits structured JSON with a `via` discriminator so callers can detect degraded mode.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1368 -- pr_merge_readiness layered fallback chain.

items.title: Structured-error contract on every failure path

narrative.Acceptance: When every fallback fails, the script still returns valid JSON on stdout shaped { error, partial_data, via }; raw stack traces never leak to stdout; stderr carries diagnostic detail.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1368 -- Structured-error contract on every failure path.

narrative.Acceptance: When invoked against a non-merged PR, the helper loops with adaptive cadence (1m / 3m / 5m), accepts fallback responses without aborting, and exits 0 when the canonical CLEAN exit fires on the underlying readiness call.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1368 -- scripts/monitor_pr.py wait-until-ready helper.

items.title: Swarm skill documents the monitor and fallback contract

narrative.Acceptance: When the swarm skill Phase 5/6 surfaces, it shows scripts/monitor_pr.py as the canonical long-running readiness monitor, documents what each `via` value emits, and rejects fallback2 as a CLEAN verdict (it is a coarse signal only).
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1368 -- Swarm skill documents the monitor and fallback contract.

items.title: Tests cover primary, fallback1, fallback2, and total-failure paths

narrative.Acceptance: When pytest runs tests/cli/test_pr_merge_readiness_fallbacks.py, it validates each of the primary, fallback1, fallback2, and total-failure paths, and asserts that each response emits the documented JSON shape, exit code, and `via` discriminator.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1368 -- Tests cover primary, fallback1, fallback2, and total-failure paths.

references.title: Issue #1368: monitoring: long-running monitors using pr_merge_readiness are unreliable in Grok Build due to harness fragility

expected_outputs: pr_merge_readiness.py emits structured JSON on every path with a `via` discriminator
scripts/monitor_pr.py loops with adaptive cadence and survives fallback responses
swarm SKILL.md documents the monitor and the fallback contract
